name: Helcim Payment API
what_it_does: Canadian payment processor with REST API offering transparent interchange-plus pricing — processing credit card payments, ACH/bank transfers, and recurring billing for North American businesses with developer API and Helcim.js for secure card collection.
use_cases: Processing credit card payments with transparent interchange-plus pricing via REST API
Collecting card details securely with Helcim.js hosted fields (no PCI scope on your server)
Managing recurring billing and subscription payments via API
ACH and bank transfer processing for US and Canadian customers
Building ecommerce checkout integrations with lower processing costs than Stripe
not_for: International payments outside North America (Helcim is Canada/USA focused)
High-risk merchant categories that major processors decline
Real-time payment initiation for complex multi-party transactions
best_when: A Canadian or US small-to-medium business wants transparent interchange-plus pricing and a developer-friendly API as a lower-cost alternative to Stripe for card processing.
avoid_when: You need global payment processing, high-risk merchant support, or non-North American payment methods.

auth.notes: API token from Helcim account. Authorization header with API key. Account-scoped access. No fine-grained scopes. Separate test/live API keys. Helcim.js uses public token for client-side card collection.

pricing.free_tier_limits: No monthly fee — pay only per transaction
pricing.paid_tiers: Interchange + 0.10% + $0.08 per transaction (online); lower for in-person
pricing.notes: No monthly fees — pay only when processing. Transparent interchange-plus pricing. Canadian company (Calgary). Cheaper than Stripe for established businesses at volume. No setup fees, no cancellation fees. 24/7 support included.

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: REST API for payments, customers, cards, and subscriptions. Helcim.js for PCI-compliant client-side collection. Webhook support for payment events. JavaScript/PHP/Python SDKs. Good documentation with practical examples.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Payment creation creates new transactions — use ecommerceId for deduplication. Subscription management idempotent. Customer management idempotent by email.
known_agent_gotchas: North America only — no international payment methods (SEPA, iDEAL, etc.)
Account approval required — not instant for all merchant categories
Helcim.js required for PCI-compliant card collection — raw API cannot accept card numbers directly
Interchange-plus pricing means costs vary by card type — harder to predict exact fees

security_score_components.security_notes: TLS enforced. PCI-DSS Level 1 compliant. Helcim.js for client-side tokenization keeps card data off merchant servers. API key auth. Canadian company with strong data protection. Test/live key separation. No raw card number API access.
